headlineSummary: Hacker News sentiment was mostly neutral-to-mixed: OpenAI leaned slightly more positive, while Google Gemini and Anthropic were close together with limited evidence on Gemini and a close tie between the two [E2][E5][E7][E8].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini came across as mixed-neutral on limited evidence, mainly because one commenter called it expensive and mediocre [E5].
Anthropic was mixed-neutral, with praise for Claude Fable's coding help balancing criticism of safe-code behavior and pricing, and it remains in a close tie with Gemini [E1][E2][E3][E4][E6].
OpenAI drew slightly more positive than negative reactions, with praise for quick fixes and usable translations offset by a note that the output was not professional quality [E5][E7][E8].

evidence.excerpt: Claude Fable 5: mid-tier results on coding tasks

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names Claude Fable 5 and characterizes its coding performance as only mid-tier.

evidence.excerpt: Just two days before I gave the same task to Codex, and the result was not really nice: it would copy the entire document to the worker thread as a snapshot for processing, and so on. Fable instead realised that it could make use of the fact that I have a self-made custom database based on operational transform running (that's why document loading is so slow :-)), and made the document processing to be just another client of that database.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Codex produced a poor solution, calling the result 'not really nice' and describing an inferior implementation.
In the Claude Fable story context, 'Fable' is credited with finding a better architectural solution than Codex.

evidence.excerpt: > Anthropic's headline cyber evaluations mostly measure offensive progress (exploits, PoCs, challenges); our benchmark tests whether a model can actually generate safe code, and there Fable 5 did not stand out. The model isn't allowed to think about security. I heard several people here mention that if it starts thinking about security -- e.g. writing tests related to it -- the safety filter flags it and downgrades to Opus. So it's actually not allowed to make your code secure.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly criticizes Anthropic's safety behavior as preventing the model from making code secure.

evidence.excerpt: In my environment and in my "style", Fable has been a huge step up, to the extent that I am seriously considering paying for a second $200/m account just to get more usage out of the next 10 days.

annotations.rationale: Within the Claude Fable story context, the commenter describes Fable as a 'huge step up' and valuable enough to justify paying for another account.

evidence.excerpt: Except for expensive and mediocre models like Gemini. In that sense, Chinese models are pretty good. I usually write code in function or method units and then design and assemble them together. GPT series models are more thorough and better, but I'm not sure if the difference is enormous.

annotations.rationale: Gemini is explicitly called 'expensive and mediocre'.
The commenter says GPT series models are 'more thorough and better,' which is mild praise despite later qualification.

evidence.excerpt: I was wondering how does Anthropic and likes keep competitive when Opus is ($5 / $25) 5x times more expensive compared to Kimi K2.6 ($0.7 / $3.4) or other Chinese models, while being only marginally better.

annotations.rationale: The comment criticizes Anthropic's value proposition as far more expensive while only marginally better.

evidence.excerpt: ChatGPT instantly responded with some speculation and then the same exact fix, with zero access to the code or a browser or anything.

annotations.rationale: The commenter praises ChatGPT for quickly producing the same fix without needing tools or code access.

evidence.excerpt: Out of curiosity, I pasted an article in French I was reading a few minutes before coming across this thread into ChatGPT and asked for a translation into English. It was certainly passable from a functional perspective, and I wouldn't hesitate to use it to translate an article from a language I don't understand. But it was not professional-quality work.

annotations.rationale: The comment gives mixed sentiment: ChatGPT is described as usable and passable, but below professional quality.
